description: Ralph-owned compute/browser resource tracking, cleanup on job outcomes, and read-only operational resource health diagnostics.

userStories.title: Add Ralph resource ownership metadata
userStories.description: As a Ralph operator, I want jobs and runs to record assigned compute resources so that retained workers and browser leases can be traced after a loop stops.
acceptanceCriteria: Add Ralph job/run resource metadata or linked records for assigned worker id, browser lease ids when known, cleanup state, retained-until time, and dirty reason
Persist resource metadata without breaking existing Ralph store migrations or older jobs
Expose resource metadata in owner-scoped Ralph list/runs JSON where appropriate
Store tests cover create, update, read, older-row compatibility, and owner-scoped access
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Ralph job/run resource metadata persistence with resource_json migrations for jobs and runs, resource normalization, owner-scoped update helpers, CLI JSON visibility through existing list/runs JSON, older-row compatibility tests, and Docker-worker validation. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused Ralph tests and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Label Ralph-owned compute workers
userStories.description: As a compute operator, I want Docker workers used by Ralph to carry job, run, and owner labels so that compute cleanup can attribute resources correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: When Ralph acquires or uses a compute worker, the worker has labels or inspectable metadata for owner scope, Ralph job id, and Ralph run id when applicable
Labels do not expose full prompt text or sensitive data
Compute list --all can display Ralph ownership fields for labeled workers
Tests cover label construction and list parsing for Ralph-owned workers
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented safe Ralph worker labels and compute list ownership visibility: Ralph job/run Docker labels are emitted only for safe id-shaped values, owner scope labels are preserved, list parsing exposes owner/worktree/Ralph fields, and `pibo compute list --all` / `--json` display the fields for labeled workers. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused compute tests and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Release browser leases after Ralph run and terminal job outcomes
userStories.description: As a Ralph user, I want browser resources released after each run or terminal job outcome so that repeated sessions do not accumulate Chromium processes.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph releases active browser leases after each run unless the next immediate run is explicitly configured to reuse the same managed browser safely
Promise-complete and max-iteration stop paths release or reap the associated browser pool
Stop request disables future runs while preserving safe cleanup after the current run finishes
Cleanup failure marks the worker or resource metadata dirty and exposes the reason
Service tests cover ok completion, promise-complete, max-iteration, stop-after-run, cleanup failure, and retained worker state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Ralph service browser-lease cleanup after run completion and terminal outcomes: run/job resource metadata is marked released after successful browser-pool release, dirty with a reason when release fails, and retained worker metadata without browser leases is preserved. Focused service tests cover ok completion, promise-complete, max-iteration, stop-after-run, cleanup failure, and retained worker state. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused Ralph tests, real local CDP release fixture, and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Apply cancel and interrupted-run resource cleanup policy
userStories.description: As an operator, I want cancelled or interrupted Ralph runs to abort browser work and mark dirty resources so that cleanup is explicit and auditable.
acceptanceCriteria: Cancel path aborts active browser leases or marks them stale when direct release is not possible
Interrupted-run recovery records resource cleanup state and dirty reason when a worker/browser lease may still exist
Prompt text cannot disable hard TTL, browser-pool reap, Docker limits, or dirty-worker recycling
Service tests cover cancel, timeout, gateway restart/interrupted recovery, and prompt text not overriding cleanup policy
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented Ralph cancel/interrupted cleanup policy: cancel emits abort for active sessions and marks resource metadata dirty if abort cannot be sent, timeout/error paths still run browser-pool lease cleanup, interrupted-run recovery marks possible worker/browser resources dirty with an auditable reason, and prompt text cannot bypass service-owned cleanup policy. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused Ralph tests, real local browser-pool release fixture, and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Expose retained resource state in Ralph CLI and API
userStories.description: As a human operator, I want disabled jobs to show retained workers or cleanup failures so that I can decide whether to inspect or reap them.
acceptanceCriteria: Ralph CLI JSON output includes resource cleanup state for jobs/runs when available
Ralph CLI text output summarizes retained worker id, retained-until time, dirty state, and next cleanup command without flooding output
Chat Web API returns resource metadata only through owner-scoped Ralph endpoints
Tests cover disabled job with retained worker, cleanup failure, no-resource legacy job, and cross-owner filtering
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented retained/dirty Ralph resource visibility in CLI/API: Ralph list/runs text now includes concise resource summaries with worker id, cleanup state, lease count, retained-until, dirty reason, and next cleanup commands; JSON output continues to include resource metadata; Chat Ralph API owner-scoped endpoints return resource metadata only for the authenticated owner. Added focused CLI/API tests covering disabled retained worker, dirty cleanup failure, legacy no-resource output, and cross-owner filtering. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused Ralph visibility tests and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Add read-only resource health diagnostics
userStories.description: As an SRE-style maintainer, I want one Pibo-native health command to detect browser leaks and Docker pressure before the host overloads.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a read-only resource health or compute doctor command that reports browser main-process count per worker, total Chromium process count, active browser leases, stale CDP files, dirty workers, OOM-killed containers, Docker disk usage, and reaper/timer status when available
Text output includes severity and concrete next commands for browser-pool reap, compute reap dry-run, Docker hygiene, or worker recycle
JSON output exposes stable fields for agents and monitoring
Default command performs no destructive cleanup
Tests cover healthy state, browser leak warning, dirty worker, OOM container, Docker disk pressure, and missing reaper/timer state
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Implemented read-only `pibo compute health` / `pibo compute doctor` with text and stable JSON fields for severity, checks, browser process counts, active browser leases, stale CDP files, dirty/OOM/cleanup-eligible workers, Docker disk pressure, reaper/timer status, and safe next commands. Added focused health tests for healthy state, browser leak warning, stale CDP/active lease, dirty worker, OOM container, Docker disk pressure, and missing reaper/timer state. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; focused tests, real read-only Docker-worker/host command validation, and full npm run typecheck passed.

userStories.title: Document and validate automatic reaper rollout
userStories.description: As an operator, I want automatic cleanup timers enabled only after dry-run validation so that production debug state is not removed unexpectedly.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the rollout sequence: resource health read-only, browser-pool reap dry-run, compute reap dry-run, manual apply, then optional timer enablement
Document recommended timer cadence and selectors for browser pool and compute worker cleanup
Document rollback steps for disabling automatic timers while keeping manual commands available
Add validation checklist entries for repeated browser checks, stopped/OOM container visibility, and Ralph terminal cleanup
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Documented the automatic reaper rollout sequence in the rollout checklist and compute capability spec: resource health read-only first, browser-pool reap validation, compute reap dry-run, manual apply, then optional timers. Added recommended browser/compute/disk cadence, selectors, rollback steps that keep manual commands available, and validation entries for repeated browser checks, stopped/OOM visibility, and Ralph terminal cleanup. Evidence recorded in IMPLEMENTATION_PROGRESS.md; documentation was validated with focused tests and full npm run typecheck.
